steps.title: Determine the four angle measures
steps.content:
When two lines intersect, vertical angles (opposite angles) are equal, and adjacent angles sum to $180°$. If one angle is $(9x - 140)°$, then its vertical angle is also $(9x - 140)°$. The two adjacent angles each measure $180 - (9x - 140) = (320 - 9x)°$.
The four angles are: $(9x - 140)°, (320 - 9x)°, (9x - 140)°, (320 - 9x)°$.

steps.title: List all possible sums of any two angles
steps.content:
From these four angles, the distinct sums are:
  • Two angles of measure $(9x - 140)°$ sum to $(18x - 280)°$
  • Two angles of measure $(320 - 9x)°$ sum to $(-18x + 640)°$
  • One angle of each measure sum to \$180°$
The possible sums are $(18x - 280)°$, $(-18x + 640)°$, and \$180°$.

steps.title: Check each choice against possible sums
steps.content:
A: $(-18x + 280)°$ — not a possible sum
B: $(-18x + 640)°$ — sum of two $(320 - 9x)°$ angles ✓
C: $(18x - 280)°$ — sum of two $(9x - 140)°$ angles ✓
D: $180°$ — sum of one angle of each type ✓

Only choice A cannot be the sum of any two angles. That matches choice A — A is correct.
